# External audit handoff package

**Project:** Fission Protocol Hedera (production rebuild)
**Repo:** `github.com/penguinpecker/fission-protocol-hedera` (public)
**Snapshot commit:** see `git rev-parse HEAD` at handoff time
**Network:** Hedera mainnet, chain ID 295
**Languages:** Solidity 0.8.27 with `via_ir = true`, `optimizer_runs = 200`,
`evm_version = cancun`, `bytecode_hash = "none"`
**Frameworks:** Foundry (test + build) + Hardhat (deploy script optional).
Hedera SDK paths used for contract creation due to the JSON-RPC value-/gas-cap
limits documented in `docs/MAINNET_DEPLOY.md` Appendix A.

This document is the single starting point for an external auditor. Read
this first; everything else (architecture, prior reviews, deploy runbook,
threat model) is linked.

---

## 1. What the protocol does

Fission Protocol is a **Pendle V2-faithful** principal/yield split AMM
deployed on Hedera. Users deposit a yield-bearing asset (HBARX, or a
SaucerSwap V2 LP NFT) into a "Standardized Yield" (SY) wrapper, then
split SY into:

- **PT** (principal token) — redeems 1 unit of underlying yield-bearing
  asset at maturity;
- **YT** (yield token) — claims the yield generated by 1 PT until
  maturity.

A specialised AMM trades PT ↔ SY along a Pendle-style curve with state
`(totalPt, totalSy, totalLp, lastLnImpliedRate, lastTradeTime)`; the
implied rate persists between trades and is the source of price
continuity. Fees go to LPs and a configurable protocol cut.

Two market flavours exist:

- **`FissionMarket`** — rate-growth pattern. PT ↔ SY redemption uses a
  global yield index. Used for SY adapters whose rate naturally grows
  (HBARX accrues stake-yield, SY rate climbs).
- **`FissionMarketRewards`** — Pendle-Kyber-style reward pattern. The
  underlying SY's `exchangeRate()` is constant `1e18`; yield arrives as
  separate **reward tokens** (e.g. WHBAR, USDC fees from a SaucerSwap
  V3 position). YT holders receive proportional reward-token payouts via
  a per-share reward index.

Liquidity providers in either flavour deposit `(SY, PT)` proportionally;
removal is symmetric. Post-expiry, `removeLiquidity` auto-redeems the
LP's PT share and pays the LP only SY (Pendle-fidelity fix from internal
pass 2 — see H-4 below).

---

## 2. In scope

| File | nSLOC | Purpose |
|------|------:|---------|
| `contracts/src/core/FissionFactory.sol`            | 178 | SY whitelist (7-day review window), market deployment, role plumbing |
| `contracts/src/core/FissionMarket.sol`             | 503 | Rate-growth AMM (HBARX-style SYs) |
| `contracts/src/core/FissionMarketRewards.sol`      | 509 | Reward-pattern AMM (SaucerSwap V2 LP-style SYs) |
| `contracts/src/core/StandardMarketDeployer.sol`    |  15 | `new FissionMarket(...)` factory shim (extracted for the 15M-gas ContractCreate cap) |
| `contracts/src/core/RewardsMarketDeployer.sol`     |  15 | `new FissionMarketRewards(...)` factory shim |
| `contracts/src/core/PrincipalToken.sol`            |  40 | HTS-native PT thin proxy |
| `contracts/src/core/YieldToken.sol`                |  45 | HTS-native YT thin proxy with `_update`-time settlement hook |
| `contracts/src/sy/SYBase.sol`                      | 148 | ERC-5115 SY core (deposit/redeem, per-share reward indexing) |
| `contracts/src/sy/SY_HBARX.sol`                    | n/a | Stader rate-bounded SY adapter (TWAP-6, 50bps cap, 1h interval, 200bps circuit breaker) |
| `contracts/src/sy/SY_SaucerSwapV2LP.sol`           | n/a | Pendle-Kyber SY: holds one fixed-range V3 LP NFT; `exchangeRate=1`; fees flow as reward tokens |
| `contracts/src/libraries/MarketMath.sol`           | 173 | Pendle V2 curve math (rateScalar, rateAnchor, executeTradeCore, addLiquidityCore, removeLiquidityCore) |
| `contracts/src/libraries/PMath.sol`                |  71 | bps math, mulDiv (round up / down), Q-format, lnWad / expWad helpers |
| `contracts/src/libraries/HtsHelpers.sol`           | n/a | HTS precompile wrappers (associate, createFungibleToken, transfer, freeze/unfreeze/wipe, idempotency) |
| `contracts/src/periphery/ActionRouter.sol`         | 204 | UUPS-upgradeable user-facing entry: depositAndSplit, swapPtSy, addLiquidityProportional, redeemAfterExpiry, HBAR↔WHBAR auto-wrap |
| `contracts/src/interfaces/*`                       | —   | Standard external contract ABIs (IStandardizedYield = ERC-5115 superset, IUniswapV3PositionManager, IStaderHBARX, IHederaTokenService, IHederaResponseCodes, IFissionMarketCommon) |

Total source nSLOC: **~2,723** (Aderyn 2026-05-06 count), build size
under `optimizer_runs = 200 / via_ir = true`.

## 3. Out of scope

- `contracts/lib/**` — OpenZeppelin, Solady, forge-std (vendored
  upstream; treat as reference unless an integration assumption is
  questioned).
- `contracts/test/**`, `contracts/script/**` — test harnesses, deploy
  scripts. Behaviour-not-shipped-to-mainnet.
- `frontend/**` — Next.js 15 App Router UI. Not in audit scope. SIWE
  auth (HS256 cookies), Supabase backend, etc., are the user-experience
  layer; auditor can ignore.
- `keeper/**` — TypeScript service that posts the keeper rate to
  `SY_HBARX`. Posts are bps-bounded by the on-chain `SY_HBARX`
  validation; auditing the keeper code is not necessary to audit the
  on-chain behaviour.

## 4. Roles, governance, admin model

| Role                        | Held by (production)                          | Powers |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|--------|
| `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE`        | OZ TimelockController (controlled by a Hedera 2-of-2 ThresholdKey account; no Gnosis Safe contract — the Hedera account enforces 2-of-2 at consensus) | Add/revoke other roles. AccessControlDefaultAdminRules with `delay=0` because the external Timelock provides delay. |
| `ADMIN_ROLE`                | Same Timelock                                  | Set protocol fee, set treasury, propose/confirm SY whitelist |
| `PAUSER_ROLE`               | Hot operator wallet                            | Pause / unpause specific markets and SYs (escape hatches always callable) |
| `KEEPER_ROLE` (`SY_HBARX`)  | Dedicated keeper EOA                           | Post a TWAP-bounded `exchangeRate` reading |

The deployer EOA must end with **zero privileged roles** on the live
system (`docs/MAINNET_DEPLOY.md` Step 8 explains the handoff sequence).

> **Current state (handoff snapshot):** Safe + Timelock have NOT been
> provisioned yet. The deployer EOA `0x32e8fd8434badbcc5d79e70e1fe0d16f86a7ab90`
> (Hedera `0.0.10463169`) currently holds every role. Phase B+C of
> `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` covers the ownership transfer; auditor
> may assume the live "post-Phase-C" model when reasoning about admin
> blast radius. If the auditor wants to verify the transfer scripts
> themselves, see `scripts/grant-keeper.mjs` and the OZ AccessControl
> ABI calls referenced in MAINNET_DEPLOY.md Step 8.

## 5. Critical invariants

These invariants are exercised in `test/invariant/*.t.sol` (8 invariants
× 256K random calls / depth 500 in CI; 0 reverts).

1. **PT solvency.**
   `totalSyHeld * globalIndex >= PT.totalSupply * 1e18 + sum(userYieldOwed) * globalIndex`
   — every PT can always redeem 1 SY at the frozen post-expiry index
   (or `≥ 1` if `globalIndex` rose between mint and expiry).
2. **AMM PT ≤ supply.** `totalPT in market <= PT.totalSupply`.
3. **LP supply consistency.** `LP.totalSupply` matches the cumulative
   add/remove sequence under any random ordering.
4. **Trade reversibility (rounding-monotone).** Round-trip swap
   `A → B → A` leaves trader at ≤ original SY (rounding always favours
   the protocol).
5. **`globalIndex` monotonic up.** Once set, never decreases (cold-start
   floor at 1e18, post-floor strict monotonic up).
6. **YT reward index monotonic up** (rewards-flavour markets).
7. **Reward conservation.** `sum_user(rewardOwed) <= total reward
   tokens received from sy.claimRewards()` over the lifetime of the
   market.
8. **Fee escrow.** Protocol fee accumulator <= cumulative reserve fee
   on swaps (no double-charge).

## 6. Threat model (what we worry about)

1. **Pendle math drift.** Cross-implementation rounding differences vs
   `PendleMarketV3` reference. `MarketMath` mirrors the reference closely
   (intentionally including the `divide-before-multiply` pattern Slither
   flags); branch coverage on `MarketMath` is 26% with the rest covered
   by Halmos symbolic specs (`test/symbolic/MarketMathSpec.t.sol`).
2. **YT yield insolvency.** A bug in the global-index machinery that
   pays YT more than the SY rate growth produced is the bug class that
   killed `~/Desktop/Projects/fission-protocol` (the v0 deploy). Fixed
   in this rebuild via the `globalIndex` floor (H-1 in pass-1) and
   `_updateGlobalIndex` floor at `PMath.ONE`.
3. **Reward-token reentrancy.** Rewards are pulled via
   `sy.claimRewards()` whose return value (not balance-delta) is
   authoritative (M-NEW-1 fix in pass-2). All harvest paths use
   `nonReentrant` and `try/catch` around `sy.claimRewards`.
4. **Penpie-style malicious SY whitelist.** `FissionFactory` enforces a
   7-day SY review window between `proposeSY` and `confirmSY`. The
   bootstrap factory deployed for the v1 smoke test had `window=0`; the
   final-factory cutover (Phase C in the runbook) re-deploys with
   `window=7 days`.
5. **HTS-token quirks.** Auto-association (HIP-904), long-zero-alias
   rejection (HIP-1217), fee-on-transfer (M-1 fix), frozen-token
   wipeTokenAccount (`_burnYt` unfreeze→wipe→refreeze fix), and the
   12 deploy gotchas in MAINNET_DEPLOY.md Appendix A.
6. **Post-expiry race.** LP exit vs PT redeemers competing for SY
   backing. Fixed in pass-2 (H-4): `removeLiquidity` post-expiry
   auto-redeems the LP's PT share, pays only SY.
7. **Donation grief.** First-deposit inflation, dust pumping, etc. —
   classified as `accepted` (cost-to-grief > damage), see Slither/Aderyn
   triage `audits/static-analysis/TRIAGE-2026-05-06.md`.
8. **Keeper rate manipulation.** TWAP-6 ring buffer + 50 bps per-update
   cap + 1h min interval + 200 bps deviation circuit breaker.
   Bidirectional (rate-down also bounded for slashing).

## 7. Prior internal reviews

- `audits/internal/SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-02.md` — pass 1: independent
  end-to-end manual + Slither/Aderyn baseline. 24 findings; H-1, H-2,
  H-3, M-2, M-3, L-6, L-10 fixed.
- `audits/internal/SECURITY_REVIEW_2026-05-02-pass2.md` — pass 2:
  Hedera-aware + attack-vector taxonomy. 9 new findings. Fixed H-4
  (LP-vs-PT post-expiry race), M-1 (fee-on-transfer), M-4 (overflow),
  M-5 (admin must be contract), M-NEW-1 (harvest reentrancy),
  L-4/L-7/L-8/L-11/L-NEW-1.

After both passes: **0 open Critical / High / Medium findings.**
Open Lows are documentation/operational only.

## 8. Static analysis

- `audits/static-analysis/slither.json` (Slither 0.11.5) +
  `slither.stderr.log`
- `audits/static-analysis/aderyn.md` (Aderyn 0.6.8)
- `audits/static-analysis/TRIAGE-2026-05-06.md` — per-finding
  classification

Net result: **0 substantive H/M findings net of triage.** All flagged
items are detector noise (reentrancy detectors that don't model
`nonReentrant`, divide-before-multiply matching Pendle reference,
strict-equality on integer counters, etc.).

## 9. Test posture

- **265 tests passing** on `main` (`forge test --no-match-path
  "test/fork/*"`). Last run: 2026-05-06.
- **Invariants:** 8 × 256K random calls, depth 500, 0 reverts.
- **Symbolic:** `test/symbolic/MarketMathSpec.t.sol` (Halmos).
- **Fork tests** against Hedera mainnet at a pinned block:
  `test/fork/SY_HBARX.fork.t.sol` (Stader rate ABI verification),
  `test/fork/SY_SaucerSwapV2LP.fork.t.sol` (NPM v3 ABI + WHBAR-USDC
  pool bytecode existence).

**Coverage (post-uplift, commit `e8b6ab8`, pre-handoff):**
| Contract                   | Branch | Notes |
|----------------------------|-------:|-------|
| FissionMarket              | 73.0%  | Pendle-style state machine; remaining gap is rare error paths |
| FissionMarketRewards       | 70.3%  | Same shape as FissionMarket + reward path |
| FissionFactory             | 60.0%  | Many branches are role-revert paths |
| ActionRouter               | 81.8%  | Periphery, hot path |
| PrincipalToken / YieldToken| 100%   | |
| MarketMath                 | 26%    | Acceptable: Halmos symbolic-execution covers the math |
| SYBase                     | 20%    | Abstract; concrete adapters carry the coverage |

**Mutation testing:** not yet run. Planned with Gambit; auditor may
request a mutation report before contracts go through full primary
audit. The user has not yet authorised the install.

## 10. Live mainnet deploy (current state)

`deployments/295.json` is the source of truth. Summary:

| Contract                | EVM address                                       | Hedera ID    | State |
|-------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|--------------|-------|
| ActionRouter            | `0x00000000000000000000000000000000009fad96`      | 0.0.10464662 | Live  |
| StandardMarketDeployer  | `0x00000000000000000000000000000000009fb0af`      | 0.0.10465455 | Live  |
| RewardsMarketDeployer   | `0x00000000000000000000000000000000009fb0b1`      | 0.0.10465457 | Live  |
| FissionFactory (BOOTSTRAP, `window=0`)  | `0x00000000000000000000000000000000009fb0b3` | 0.0.10465459 | Live (smoke-test only — replaced in Phase C) |
| SY_SaucerSwapV2LP       | `0x00000000000000000000000000000000009fb089`      | 0.0.10465417 | Live, post-fix |
| SY_HBARX (BROKEN, predates fixes) | `0x80728fbad79974e428c50dc548853ff858d9430c` | 0.0.10464740 | DO NOT USE — replaced in Phase C |
| Market 0 (V2 LP rewards, 90d)     | `0xfa903b938b3bbb0d2836010e5f45edc95fd08a6d` |              | Live, smoke-test seed (~$0.10/side) |

Phase C (final-factory cutover) replaces the bootstrap factory and
SY_HBARX with post-fix versions and seeds production-sized markets. See
`docs/MAINNET_DEPLOY.md` Appendix B.

## 11. What to focus on (auditor's eye)

1. **`FissionMarket.sol` + `FissionMarketRewards.sol`** — the AMM core.
   Pendle math is well-understood reference; bugs here usually live in
   the *integration* (yield-index update timing, post-expiry redemption
   path, reward-index update timing).
2. **`SY_SaucerSwapV2LP.sol`** — V3 NFT custody + reward harvesting +
   `nonReentrant`-protected reentrancy paths. The `npm.mint{value:...}`
   forwarding pattern (Hedera bug fix #8) is unusual and worth
   challenging.
3. **`HtsHelpers.sol`** — every HTS precompile interaction. The
   freeze/unfreeze/wipe sequence in `_burnYt` (bug #9) is unusual; the
   3-way `setTokens` `msg.value/3` split (bug #4) is unusual; the
   `setMaxAutomaticTokenAssociations(-1)` reliance (bug #6) is
   Hedera-specific.
4. **`FissionFactory.proposeSY` / `confirmSY`** — make sure the 7-day
   window is enforced under any clock-skew or admin-rotation scenario.
5. **`ActionRouter.sol`** — reentrancy surface is large (HBAR↔WHBAR
   wrap + multi-token approve + market entry). UUPS upgradeability —
   verify storage layout has no collisions.

## 12. What we already accept as residual

Documented in pass-1 / pass-2 reviews + static-analysis triage:

- Slither divide-before-multiply on `MarketMath.executeTradeCore` etc.
  (matches Pendle reference; changing breaks invariants).
- `block.timestamp` granularity at expiry boundary
  (test_expiryBoundary_* covers).
- Donation/dust grief into `harvestRewards` (griefer pays real tokens
  to benefit other users; not a vulnerability).
- `_settleUserRewards(address(this))` skip — silent dust-stuck path
  patched but the address(this) skip itself is residual.
- HIP-1217 long-zero ECDSA alias rejection — operational checklist in
  the deploy runbook.

## 13. How to bring this up to a primary audit firm

Suggested two-step:

1. **Pre-audit / scoping (HashEx or Hacken).** ~$15-30K, 1-2 weeks. Goal:
   confirm scope, identify anything obviously wrong before paying for a
   primary firm's hours.
2. **Primary (ChainSecurity or Spearbit).** Address every finding before
   moving on. Expect 4-6 weeks engagement, $80-150K.
3. **Public contest (Code4rena or Sherlock).** Address.
4. **Bug bounty (Immunefi).** Live before mainnet user-funds.

Total budget per `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`: ~$280-380K, ~4 months.

## 14. Reproducible build

```sh
cd contracts
forge --version    # 1.5.1+
forge build
forge test --no-match-path "test/fork/*"
```

Expected: 265 tests pass, 0 fail, 0 skip.

For static analysis:

```sh
# venv first time
python3 -m venv .venv-slither
.venv-slither/bin/pip install slither-analyzer solc-select
.venv-slither/bin/solc-select install 0.8.27 && .venv-slither/bin/solc-select use 0.8.27

# Run
export PATH=$HOME/.foundry/bin:$PWD/.venv-slither/bin:$PATH
cd contracts && slither . --json ../audits/static-analysis/slither.json \
  --filter-paths "lib/|test/|script/"
cd .. && aderyn --output audits/static-analysis/aderyn.md
```

Build env caveats (Foundry):

- `via_ir = true` is required (some functions hit "stack too deep"
  without it).
- `bytecode_hash = "none"` and `cbor_metadata = false` are set for
  reproducibility — but this is what blocks Sourcify full-match.
  Auditors should verify against the local `out/` artifacts directly,
  not via Sourcify.

## 15. Contact + deliverables we want back

- All findings with severity (Critical / High / Medium / Low / Info /
  Acknowledged) and a recommended fix or accept-with-rationale.
- Re-test on the patch commits after fixes.
- A retest letter / final report attesting to the post-fix state.

Operator contact: `kaboomweb3@gmail.com` (project owner).
